description:
You are acting as the “configuration-manager” account on a Linux workstation (home directory: /home/user).  
 A historical change-tracking log already exists at  
    /home/user/logs/config_changes.log  

 Your goal is to create a **filtered copy** that contains only the lines which represent files that were actually modified, i.e. those whose status tag is the word **“CHANGED”** (in all-caps).  
 The resulting file must be written to  

    /home/user/output/summary.log  

 Output-format requirements (these will be checked byte-for-byte):  
 1. Keep every CHANGED line exactly as it appears in the source log (identical whitespace, timestamp, path, etc.).  
 2. Preserve the original line-ordering.  
 3. Do **not** add any extra header, footer, blank lines, or text.  
 4. The file must end with a single Unix newline character (`\n`).  

 Important implementation notes for you as the AI assistant:  
 • Accomplish the filtering with a single terminal command that relies on a regular expression match for the literal string “CHANGED” as an independent word (case-sensitive).  
 • Be sure that /home/user/output/ exists before you try to write the summary file; create it if necessary with normal user permissions.  
 • When finished, you may cat the resulting summary file to the console so we can see the final content, but the file itself is what will be graded.
truth:
Existing file before the task starts:  

   Path: /home/user/logs/config_changes.log  
   Mode: 0644 (rw-r--r--)  
   Contents (exact, with trailing newline at the end of the last line):  
[2023-05-01 09:15:23] CHANGED: /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
[2023-05-01 09:15:23] BACKUP: /etc/nginx/nginx.conf.bak
[2023-05-01 09:18:47] CHANGED: /etc/ssh/sshd_config
[2023-05-01 09:20:02] SKIPPED: /etc/hosts
[2023-05-01 09:21:10] CHANGED: /etc/php/php.ini
[2023-05-01 09:25:30] DRYRUN: /etc/fstab
[2023-05-01 09:27:54] CHANGED: /etc/systemd/system.conf
 (note: there is a single `\n` at the very end after the last line)  

 The agent must create the directory /home/user/output/ if it does not already exist (permissions 0755 are sufficient) and then create the filtered file:  

   Path: /home/user/output/summary.log  
   Mode: 0644  
   Expected contents after successful completion (exact):  
[2023-05-01 09:15:23] CHANGED: /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
[2023-05-01 09:18:47] CHANGED: /etc/ssh/sshd_config
[2023-05-01 09:21:10] CHANGED: /etc/php/php.ini
[2023-05-01 09:27:54] CHANGED: /etc/systemd/system.conf
 (again, terminated with a single trailing newline)
